solution_metadata.competition: Flavours of Physics: Finding tau to 3mu
solution_metadata.team: Tatiana Likhomanenko & Alexey Rogozhnikov (Yandex Data School)
solution_metadata.source_license: NO LICENSE (blocked for ingestion; hep_ml is Apache-2.0)

evidence_summary.resolved_reason: Domain-specific physics knowledge: which features are guaranteed mass-independent. Modeled as external_knowledge concept_type. Not a gap — the knowledge is problem-specific and cannot be generalized into an atom.

evidence_summary.binding_rationale: The novel component (flatness penalty loss function) is now an atom. The boosting structure itself uses standard GBC/XGBoost. Bound via callable_injection: the loss atom is passed as a custom objective to the boosting trainer. Confidence 0.85 because the full stage is a composition (loss atom + boosting trainer), not a single atom.

architect_test_expectations.description: All 6 stages are gaps. This CDG tests the architect's handling of constraint gates (KS, CvM) that act as hard pass/fail filters before metric computation.
architect_test_expectations.critical_finding: The constraint-gate pattern (hard statistical tests as acceptance criteria) is broadly applicable to fairness-aware ML. The architect should recognize that compute_ks_agreement and compute_cvm_mass_decorrelation are evaluation/auditing atoms, not model components. The NO LICENSE status blocks direct ingestion but clean-room reimplementation from papers is feasible for all atoms.
architect_test_expectations.expected_retrieval_path: No atoms should be found. All stages should be reported as gaps. The flatness_constrained_gradient_boosting atom may be partially matchable to hep_ml (Apache 2.0) if that library is indexed.
